title: xanthophyllum ecarinatum
intro: Xanthophyllum ecarinatum, also known as the "yellow wood tree", is a plant species belonging to the family Polygalaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Xanthophyllum ecarinatum include its potential to treat fever, headaches, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
